figures.caption: Figure 2. Performance of a Shared Autoencoder Model Across Datasets and 490 Modalities. (a) Final test reconstruction error for RNA and morphology autoencoders 491 across four datasets (LUAD, LINCS, CDRP-bio, TAORF). Bars report modality-specific 492 reconstruction error evaluated on held-out test data, highlighting dataset- and modality-493 dependent reconstruction performance. (b) Box plots show the distribution of R² values 494 across morphology features for each dataset, evaluated using 5-fold cross-validation of the 495 shared latent-space model. Black points and error bars indicate the mean R² and 95% 496 bootstrap confidence interval. Green triangles denote median R² values reported for 497 multilayer perceptron models in prior work7 for comparison. (c) Bar plot showing the 498 proportion of morphology features within each subcategory achieving a median R² > 0.6 499 across all datasets. Subcategories related to AGP-RadialDistribution, Mito-500 RadialDistribution, and ER-Texture exhibited the strongest transcriptomic predictability. 501

Figure 1. Schematic of multimodal data integration and cross-modal prediction 481 using a shared autoencoder architecture. For each dataset, separate autoencoders are 482 trained for gene expression and morphology data, each with a modality-specific encoder 483 and decoder. The learned representations from each modality are embedded into a shared 484 latent space, enabling integration across datasets and supporting cross-modal prediction 485 such as inferring morphology profiles from gene expression data using the shared 486 representation. 487

Figure 5. Convergence between predictive genes and genetically supported 525 expression–morphology associations. (a) Overlap between genes prioritized by the 526 shared autoencoder and genes identified by gene-level expression-morphology 527 association analyses across top morphology features. Points show TWAS effect sizes (β) 528 for the association between genetically regulated gene expression and morphology, 529 restricted to genes with cis-eQTL support. Diamonds denote genes identified by both 530 approaches. Associations are shown for genes passing false discovery rate correction (q 531 < 0.05) within each morphology feature; the dashed line indicates β = 0. (b) Representative 532 variant–gene–morphology chains linking genetic variation to morphology through gene 533 expression. Each schematic depicts a significant cis-eQTL effect (variant → gene 534 expression; β ± SE) followed by a significant TWAS effect (genetically regulated expression 535

Figure 4. Distributed transcriptional prediction and CRISPR validation of 513 representative gene–morphology pairs. (a,d) Top 20 genes ranked by model-derived 514 importance scores for prediction of Cells_RadialDistribution_RadialCV_AGP_1of4 (a) and 515 Cells_AreaShape_Zernike_4_2 (d). Relative coefficient magnitudes indicate distributed 516 contributions across multiple genes rather than dominance by a single predictor. (b,e) 517 Scatter plots of individual gene expression versus morphology for representative top-518 ranked genes, TIAM1 (b) and RAB31 (e), demonstrate weak marginal correlations despite 519 high model importance. Shaded regions indicate 95% confidence intervals. (c,f) 520 Independent CRISPR perturbation validation using JUMP Cell Painting data. Disruption of 521 TIAM1 significantly alters AGP radial distribution (c), and disruption of RAB31 significantly 522 shifts AreaShape Zernike features (f) relative to plate-matched non-targeting controls. 523

Figure 3. Comparison of global and individual-level morphology prediction 503 accuracy. Scatter plot showing R² values for individual morphology features, comparing 504 global model predictions (y-axis) to the mean prediction accuracy across 100 individual-505 level evaluations (x-axis). Most features cluster near or above the diagonal (y = x) line, 506 indicating consistent predictive performance between global and individual evaluations. 507 Highlighted features satisfy R² > 0.6 in both global and mean individual evaluations and 508 pass BH-FDR correction for global predictability (q < 0.05; permutation test). 509
